title: Ensure that 'Subscription leaving Microsoft Entra tenant' and  'Subscription entering Microsoft Entra tenant' is set to 'Permit no  one'

input_row.title: Ensure that 'Subscription leaving Microsoft Entra tenant' and  'Subscription entering Microsoft Entra tenant' is set to 'Permit no  one'
input_row.description: Users who are set as subscription owners are able to make administrative changes to the subscriptions and move them into and out of Microsoft Entra ID.
input_row.rationale: Permissions to move subscriptions in and out of a Microsoft Entra tenant must only be given to appropriate administrative personnel. A subscription that is moved into a Microsoft Entra tenant may be within a folder to which other users have elevated permissions. This prevents loss of data or unapproved changes of the objects within by potential bad actors.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From the Azure Portal Home select the portal menu 2. Select Subscriptions 3. In the Advanced options drop-down menu, select Manage Policies 4. Ensure Subscription leaving Microsoft Entra tenant and Subscription entering Microsoft Entra tenant are set to Permit no one
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From the Azure Portal Home select the portal menu 2. Select Subscriptions 3. In the Advanced options drop-down menu, select Manage Policies 4. Set Subscription leaving Microsoft Entra tenant and Subscription entering Microsoft Entra tenant to Permit no one 5. Click Save changes
input_row.impact: Subscriptions will need to have these settings turned off to be moved.
input_row.default_value: By default Subscription leaving Microsoft Entra tenant and Subscription entering Microsoft Entra tenant are set to Allow everyone (default)

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Log in to the AWS Management Console** and navigate to the **AWS Organizations** service.
2. Select the **Accounts** menu to view all AWS accounts linked to your organization.
3. Verify the **organization settings** related to service control policies, since there is no direct mapping to Azure's subscription policies.
4. Check the membership of accounts to any organizational units to understand if they are within a broader permission structure.
5. **Document any deviations** from desired settings or access permissions as these will relate to the movement of accounts/subscriptions.
6. For individual IAM roles or users that may have restricted permissions on moving accounts, ensure you review the **IAM policies** assigned to those accounts to make sure they are not overly permissive.
7. **Expected secure configuration** involves ensuring no IAM entities (users, roles) are permitted to move accounts/subscriptions frequently or without adequate approval.
8. **Confirm compliance** by reviewing documentation on account movements, and if available, the logs of IAM policy changes related to account movement permissions.

In this case, checking permissions and privileges related to account movements in AWS requires human judgment because AWS does not have a straightforward API to check for subscription movements, as this is very differently structured from Azure’s subscription model. Therefore, manual checking is necessary.
